chapter_title.ar: باب الزَّكَاةِ تُبْعَثُ مِنْ بَلَدٍ إِلَى بَلَدٍ أَوْ تُدْفَعُ إِلَى مَنْ يَقْسِمُهَا فَتَضِيعُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – The Zakāt being sent from a city to a city, or is handed over to someone to distribute it, so it gets wasted

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/101)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/101)
text: هُ قَالَ إِذَا أَخْرَجَهَا مِنْ مَالِهِ فَذَهَبَتْ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّهَا لِأَحَدٍ فَقَدْ بَرِئَ مِنْهَا .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When he extracts it (Zakāt) from his wealth, so it gets wasted, and he had not specified it for anyone, so he is not accountable for it’.
en.sarwar: 3. Hariz has narrated from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one takes away Zakat from his assets but does not name the beneficiary and it is lost, he is free from his obligation.’”
